overview: This structure appears to be a Balanophora species, a type of parasitic flowering plant that closely resembles a mushroom. It lacks chlorophyll and does not perform photosynthesis, instead taking nutrients directly from tree roots. The plant is often found in dark forest undergrowth and can have an acorn-like appearance. ScienceAlert +2 3 sites This Bizarre Parasitic 'Mushroom' Plant Quit Photosynthesis Dec 29, 2025 — "Balanophora has lost much of what defines it as a plant, but retained enough to function as a parasite," says botanist Petra Svet...

The plant is often found in dark forest undergrowth and can have an acorn-like appearance.
